error.signature: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cuda:0 and cpu

verdict.summary: Tensors on different devices (CPU vs GPU) used in same operation. Model on GPU but input on CPU or vice versa.

dead_ends.action: Move every tensor to GPU at creation time
dead_ends.why_fails: Some tensors (like labels, indices) should stay on CPU until needed. Eager .cuda() wastes VRAM.
.cuda() hardcodes GPU and breaks on CPU-only machines or multi-GPU. Use .to(device) consistently.

workarounds.action: Use a device variable and .to(device) consistently
workarounds.how: device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'); model.to(device); x = x.to(device)

workarounds.action: Check both model and data are on the same device before forward pass
Move criterion/loss function to device if it has parameters
